emergency_definition: Fire suppression system is completely offline

emergency_routing_rules.fallback: Fallback chain: Danny Rivera (813-555-0147) -> Mike Johnson (813-555-0199) -> Backup (813-555-0100)

non_emergency_routing_rules.after_hours: Collect caller information; Get name and callback number; Promise callback next business day

call_transfer_rules.failure_message: I apologize, I wasn't able to connect you. Let me take your information and someone will call you back shortly.

integration_constraints: Never create sprinkler jobs in ServiceTrade automatically
Sarah Miller: Also, fire alarm jobs can be auto-created in ServiceTrade for non-emergency requests
special_rules: Mike Johnson: If someone mentions a fire department inspection or a fire marshal visit, flag that as high priority even if it's not an emergency
greeting: Thank you for calling FireShield Protection Services. This is Clara, how may I help you today?
after_hours_flow_summary:
1. Greet caller
2. Ask purpose of call
3. Determine if emergency or non-emergency
4. Emergency triggers: Sprinkler head discharge, Active fire alarm, Fire pump failure...
5. Emergency: collect name, number, address -> transfer chain: Danny Rivera (813-555-0147) -> Mike Johnson (813-555-0199)
6. If transfer fails: take message, assure callback
7. Non-emergency: collect info, promise callback next business day
8. Ask 'Is there anything else I can help with?'
9. Close call
office_hours_flow_summary:
1. Greet caller
2. Ask purpose of call
3. Collect caller name and callback number
4. Transfer to: None (813-555-0100)
5. If transfer fails: take message, confirm follow-up
6. Ask 'Is there anything else I can help with?'
7. Close call
notes: Auto-extracted from onboarding call transcript for FireShield Protection Services. Emergency contact chain: 2 contact(s) configured. Services: 6 identified. Emergency triggers: 4 defined. Flagged 1 unknown(s) for human review. Special handling rules: 1 captured.
